title: Data transmission with interleaved mapping for high carrier frequency
abstract: Various embodiments herein provide techniques related to transmission of a physical shared channel such as a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) or a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH). Specifically, the transmission may use a Discrete Fourier Transform-spread- orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (DFT-s-OFDM) waveform that is related to an identified index such as an orthogonal cover code (OCC) index or a comb index. Other embodiments may be described and/or claimed.
claims: Claims 1. An electronic device for use in a fifth generation (5G) nodeB (gNB) in a 5G cellular network, wherein the electronic device comprises: one or more processors; and one or more non-transitory computer-readable media comprising instructions that, upon execution of the instructions by the one or more processors, are to cause the gNB to: identify an index related to a transmission of a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH) with a Discrete Fourier Transform-spread- orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (DFT-s-OFDM) waveform; encode the PDSCH based on the identified index; and transmit the encoded PDSCH to a user equipment (UE) of the 5G cellular network. 2. The electronic device of claim 1, wherein the transmission of the encoded PDSCH is on a frequency resource that is at or above 52.6 gigahertz (GHz). 3. The electronic device of claim 1, wherein the instructions are further to cause the gNB to transmit an indication of the index to the UE. 4. The electronic device of any of claims 1-3, wherein the index is a comb index, and wherein the instructions to encode the PDSCH based on the comb index include instructions to: modulate scrambled symbols of the PDSCH to generate modulated symbols; perform a DFT operation to generate transformed symbols; and map, based on the comb index, the transformed symbols to one or more transmission physical resources. 5. The electronic device of any of claims 1-3, wherein the index is an orthogonal cover code (OCC) index, and wherein the instructions to encode the PDSCH based on the identified OCC index include instructions to: modulate scrambled symbols of the PDSCH to generate modulated symbols; spread, based on the identified index, the modulated symbols by a block-wised OCC operation to generate spread symbols; perform a DFT operation on the spread symbols to generate transformed symbols; and map the transformed symbols to one or more transmission physical resources. 36 6. The electronic device of claim 5, wherein the one or more transmission resources are interleaved subcarriers. 7. The electronic device of claim 6, wherein the interleaved subcarriers are allocated in every M subcarrier. 8. The electronic device of claim 7, wherein the instructions are further to cause the gNB to transmit an indication of the value of M in remaining minimum system information (RMSI), NR other system information (OSI), radio resource control (RRC) signaling, or downlink control information (DCI). 9. The electronic device of claim 5, wherein the instructions to encode the PDSCH based on the identified index include instructions to insert a phase-tracking reference-signal with data of the PDSCH prior to the spread of the modulated symbols. 10. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the PT-RS is based on a PT-RS pattern that is determined based on an allocated bandwidth or a spreading factor. 11. An electronic device to be used in a user equipment (UE) of a fifth generation (5G) cellular network, wherein the electronic device comprises: one or more processors; and one or more non-transitory computer-readable media comprising instructions that, upon execution of the instructions by the one or more processors, are to cause the UE to: identify, in a transmission received from a 5G nodeB (gNB) of the cellular network, an indication of an index related to a transmission of a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) with a Discrete Fourier Transform-spread- orthogonal frequencydivision multiplexing (DFT-s-OFDM) waveform; encode the PUSCH based on the identified index; and transmit the encoded PUSCH to the gNB. 12. The electronic device of claim 11, wherein the transmission of the encoded PUSCH is on a frequency resource that is at or above 52.6 gigahertz (GHz). 37 13. The electronic device of claims 11 or 12, wherein the index is a comb index, and the instructions to encode the PUSCH based on the comb index include instructions to: modulate scrambled symbols of the PUSCH to generate modulated symbols; perform a DFT operation to generate transformed symbols; and map, based on the comb index, the transformed symbols to one or more transmission physical resources. 14. The electronic device of claims 11 or 12, wherein the index is an orthogonal cover code (OCC) index, and the instructions to encode the PUSCH based on the identified OCC index include instructions to: modulate scrambled symbols of the PUSCH to generate modulated symbols; spread, based on the identified index, the modulated symbols by a block-wised OCC operation to generate spread symbols; perform a DFT operation on the spread symbols to generate transformed symbols; and map the transformed symbols to one or more transmission resources. 15. The electronic device of claim 14, wherein the one or more transmission resources are interleaved subcarriers. 16. The electronic device of claim 15, wherein the interleaved subcarriers are allocated in every M subcarrier. 17. The electronic device of claim 16, wherein the value of M is based on an indication received in remaining minimum system information (RMSI), NR other system information (OSI), radio resource control (RRC) signaling, or downlink control information (DCI). 18. The electronic device of claim 14, wherein the instructions to encode the PDSCH based on the identified index include instructions to insert a phase-tracking reference-signal with data of the PDSCH prior to the spreading. 19. The electronic device of claim 18, wherein the PT-RS is based on a PT-RS pattern that is determined based on an allocated bandwidth or a spreading factor. 20. One or more non-transitory computer-readable media comprising instructions that, upon execution of the instructions by one or more processors of an electronic device of a fifth generation (5G) cellular network, are to cause the electronic device to: identify an index related to a transmission of a physical shared channel with a Discrete Fourier Transform-spread-orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (DFT-x-OFDM) waveform; encode the physical shared channel based on the identified index; and facilitate transmission of the encoded physical shared channel to another electronic device of the 5G cellular network. 21. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 20, wherein the physical shared channel is a physical uplink shared channel (PUSCH) or a physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH). 22. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claims 20 or 21, wherein the index is an orthogonal cover code (OCC) index or a comb index.
